message:
fix(scripts): strengthen prompt ID validation to reject trailing hyphens

The previous regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ would accept IDs like "my-prompt-"
with trailing hyphens. These are problematic because:
1. They create ugly URLs: /prompts/my-prompt-/
2. They violate kebab-case conventions
3. They can cause issues with some URL parsers

Changes:
- Update regex to ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z0-9]+)*$
- This requires each hyphen-separated segment to have content after it
- Valid: "my-prompt", "a-b-c", "test123"
- Invalid: "my-prompt-", "-prompt", "my--prompt"

Also updates the error message to explicitly mention "no trailing hyphens"
for clearer CI failure diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
